summary: A single-story rectangular building with a distinctive stepped pyramid roof. The structure features a wood plank wall with four evenly-spaced windows, a stonebrick foundation and base platform, and a dark wood shingle roof with multiple stepped tiers. The building sits on a raised dirt foundation surrounded by a stonebrick perimeter.

shape.overall_form: rectangular box with stepped pyramid roof

elements: four rectangular windows with glass panes
stepped pyramid roof with multiple tiers
rebuild_hints: Create a 10×8 rectangular footprint at ground level
Build a 1-block-high stonebrick foundation platform around the perimeter
Construct walls from wood planks, approximately 3 blocks high
Place four evenly-spaced windows (approximately 2×2 blocks each) on the front and back faces
Add fence posts at corners and between windows for structural detail
Build the roof as a stepped pyramid: start with a full-width layer, then step inward by 1 block on each side for each successive layer
Use dark wood blocks (dark oak or similar) for all roof tiers
The roof should have approximately 4-5 stepped layers reaching a peak
Surround the building with a stonebrick border at ground level
uncertainties: Exact window dimensions and spacing cannot be precisely determined from angles
Exact number of roof tiers is approximately 4-5 but may vary by 1
Whether windows have frames or are simple glass panes
Exact wood type (oak, spruce, etc.) cannot be definitively determined
Depth dimension estimated from perspective; could be 7-9 blocks
